data.title: Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA) Slides as NFL Network Battle Enters Third Month
data.description:
Time is running out for entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA) to establish a carriage deal with the NFL Network. And with competitors circling, Comcast needs the NFL haven on its side now more than ever as football season approaches. Investors seem to be getting quite concerned, based on the nearly 3% loss in Comcast share prices seen in Thursday morning's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The NFL Network has been off limits to Comcast Xfinity subscribers since May, and there are no signs that the NFL Network will relent any time soon. The one saving grace for Comcast subscribers right now is that the NFL Network's value to subscribers is comparatively limited, since we are in football's offseason right now. That being said, Xfinity subscribers will certainly notice the lack a lot more if Week One fires up—preseason play starts in early August—and NFL Network is not on hand.

Both sides are at something of an impasse. NFL Network is currently owned by ESPN, a Disney (DIS) property. Comcast asserts that Disney is simply asking for too much money, and that Xfinity subscribers cannot bear the load. But Disney rebuffs that, noting that it offered to allow Comcast to continue airing NFL Network while negotiations ran, but Comcast refused.

Another Game Studio

Meanwhile, reports suggest that Comcast breaking off from NBCUniversal may result in a new video game studio emerging. NBCUniversal may launch its own line of video games following the split, which is not especially surprising given the sheer amount of intellectual property under its umbrella. While this certainly means a ton of Minions-related games, there is a possibility that NBCUniversal can mine its horror classics for content as well. An age-appropriate Dracula game could be a winner; just ask all four seasons of Castlevania.

A split could prove an attractive takeover target, thanks to film and television studios as well as theme parks and the Peacock streaming service. Netflix (NFLX) might well consider such a move after losing out on Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD).

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on six Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.07% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $33.00 per share implies 42.67% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Is SoFi Stock Worth Buying on Today's Dip? One Analyst Sees a More than 65% Upside for the Shares
data.description:
SoFi (SOFI) stock was down on Thursday, and investors might be wondering if now is the time to buy into the fintech company. Turning to Wall Street, one analyst believes SOFI stock still has plenty of room to run. Citi analyst Peter Christiansen holds the highest price target for SOFI stock among his peers at $30. This price target suggests a possible 66.48% upside for the shares. It also goes without saying that the analyst's Street-high price target comes with a Buy rating.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Why Such a Bullish Stance on SoFi Stock?

Christiansen updated his coverage of SoFi after the company's Q1 earnings report on April 29, 2026. Investors will remember that the fintech company posted earnings per share that matched Wall Street's estimates at 12 cents. Its revenue of $1.41 billion was also well above analysts' average estimate of $1.05 billion.

What's interesting about the Citi analyst's price target for SOFI stock is that he cut it after earnings. Before SoFi revealed its Q1 results, Christiansen had a $37 price target for the shares. He cited compression in the lending technology space as the reason for the lowered price target. Despite that price target reduction, the analyst praised the strong Q1 results and remained bullish on the shares.

SoFi Stock Movement Today

SoFi stock was down 1.95% on Thursday, extending a 30.52% year-to-date fall. The shares have also dropped 0.7% over the past 12 months.

SOFI stock trading activity today was light, as some 35.7 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume was about 73.82 million shares.

Considering today's dip in share price and Christiansen's bullish coverage, traders might think it's a good time to buy SOFI stock. Before they go running off to buy shares, they might want to consider the wider analysts' consensus on the company.

Is SoFi Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for SoFi is Hold, based on six Buy, 10 Hold, and three Sell ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average SOFI stock price target of $20.69, which suggests a possible 13.67% upside for the shares. (See SOFI's Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: 'It's a Gift': Why D.A. Davidson Has Changed its Tone on Palantir Stock
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) stock has disappointed investors this year. Even after staging a comeback recently, the shares still sit 26.5% below 2026's starting point.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The downbeat sentiment has been driven by concerns over a very lofty valuation and the threat posed by frontier AI labs like Anthropic and OpenAI, with the argument being that these new entrants will make software companies obsolete.

However, with the share price now at such a depressed level, D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria believes investors can make the most of an exceptional opportunity. "Palantir has grown into its valuation as profits have soared, and the multiple has come in, providing investors with a gift just in time for the United States of America's 250th birthday," Luria said.

In fact, Luria believes Palantir's valuation is the "most attractive it has been in a while," especially when compared to other high-growth software companies. "While PLTR now trades in line with other AI-positive standouts SNOW, SHOP, DDOG and CRWD, it is growing twice as fast as any of them," he said.

Accordingly, Luria upgraded his PLTR rating from Neutral to Buy and raised his price target from $165 to $175. Should the figure be met, investors will be pocketing returns of 35% a year from now. (To watch Luria's track record, click here)

Luria believes that in the AI era, Palantir's core strength – bringing together scattered data sources without losing integrity, privacy, or security – has become "even more important."

It helps speed up AI deployment by solving a major sticking point: getting all the underlying data into one place with proper governance and accuracy. Many IT teams are realizing this is doable with hyperscaler and analytics tools, but it tends to take years and often frustrates business users along the way. "Palantir had the right strength at the right time," he says.

Meanwhile, recent developments "illustrate precisely" why customers will use Palantir for AI orchestration. Anthropic's repeated confrontational stance toward the US government has led to regulatory restrictions on certain AI models, with Anthropic even removing its own models from the market for a period (restrictions have now been eased). For an enterprise building directly on top of those models, that kind of disruption could be "catastrophic."

By contrast, a company built on an orchestration layer like Palantir would face a much smaller impact, since Palantir can switch out the underlying models while keeping the rest of the system intact. This weakens the main perceived risk to Palantir – that customers would bypass it and go directly to Anthropic or OpenAI for critical solutions.

Even if frontier model providers build deployment companies and hire strong forward-deployed engineers, customers are likely to remain cautious about tying their core systems to a single model provider. There is also the added uncertainty that today's leading model may not stay state-of-the-art and could eventually be replaced by cheaper open-source alternatives.

So, that's the D.A. Davidson view, but what does the rest of the Street have in mind for Palantir? Based on an additional 13 Buys, 4 Holds and 2 Sells, the analyst consensus rates the stock a Moderate Buy. At $181.63, the average price target points toward 12-month returns of 40.5%. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Lululemon (LULU) Hit with Consumer Lawsuit Over Tariff Price Increases
data.description:
More bad news for Canadian athletic apparel maker Lululemon Athletica (LULU).

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The Vancouver-based retailer has been hit with a consumer lawsuit for raising prices on its yoga pants and sneakers to help offset costs associated with U.S. tariffs. The lawsuit accuses Lululemon of violating U.S. consumer protection laws over its tariff-linked price increases that have occurred in the past year.

Specifically, the lawsuit claims that Lululemon has failed to provide refunds to customers after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's tariffs, invalidating the duties. Lululemon makes most of its clothing and shoes in Asia and was heavily impacted by U.S. tariffs. The company has estimated that tariffs cost it $275 million in 2025.

The Status of Tariff Refunds

Like many companies, Lululemon has filed a lawsuit in the U.S. Court of International Trade to recover hundreds of millions of dollars in tariffs that it paid over the past year. However, the company has yet to receive any compensation from the Trump administration after the Supreme Court ruling.

The status of tariff refunds is largely up in the air, with some companies accusing the U.S. government of dragging its feet on reimbursing companies. The Trump administration has filed a legal appeal regarding which companies should receive tariff refunds.

For Lululemon Athletica, the consumer lawsuit is yet another headache for management. The company recently resolved its years-long proxy battle with founder Chip Wilson and managed to get shareholders to approve its slate of new board members. But now, it is facing a potentially bruising lawsuit from its customers.

Is LULU Stock a Buy?

Lululemon Athletica's stock has a consensus Hold rating among 21 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on one Buy and 20 Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LULU price target of $167.38 implies 34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: HOOD Stock Jumps Over 8% on New Robinhood Blockchain and Guinness World Record AI Stunt
data.description:
HOOD stock (HOOD) jumped 8.4% to trade around $108 today following a major keynote event in London. Robinhood caught Wall Street's attention by officially launching its own blockchain network, known as the Robinhood Chain, to the public.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

To celebrate the launch, Robinhood pulled off an on-stage stunt where an artificial intelligence agent used a virtual credit card to complete the most purchases within three minutes, setting a brand-new Guinness World Record. The move highlights the big plans Robinhood has to let AI tools trade stocks, options, and crypto for users in the future.

Details of Robinhood's New Blockchain

The newly launched Robinhood Chain is built using Arbitrum technology, which processes financial tasks off-chain before settling them directly onto the secure Ethereum network to keep transaction fees very low.

Instead of creating a custom cryptocurrency to run the network, Robinhood decided to use standard Ethereum (ETH-USD) to pay for transaction costs. The main goal of this new chain is to support tokenized real-world assets and financial services.

As part of the launch, eligible users across more than 120 countries can now use the Robinhood Wallet to trade tokenized stocks 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The network is also launching with heavy support from major decentralized finance partners like Uniswap and Chainlink.

Robinhood's Crypto Expansion Lifts Wall Street sentiment

Beyond international stock trading, Robinhood is also building out new ways for users to grow their crypto holdings.

Through a new partnership, Robinhood is letting eligible U.S. users lend out stablecoins to earn an estimated 7% annual yield. This major expansion into the digital asset space helped HOOD stock turn around cautious analyst reports from earlier this year that warned slow crypto trading volumes could drag the company down.

While investors reacted very favorably to the news today, the focus for Robinhood will now shift to how many developers and traders actually choose to use the network over the coming weeks.

Is HOOD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HOOD stock based on 16 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average 12-month HOOD price target of $105.17 per share implies 8.9% downside risk. (See HOOD stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: S&P 500 Faces Risk of a 'Three-Wave Correction,' Warns Bank of America
data.description:
Paul Ciana, Bank of America's Global Head of Technical Strategy, believes that the S&P 500 (SPX) could face a "three-wave correction" in the coming months. A three-wave correction is a decline that occurs in three stages before the market stabilizes. The correction could bring the index to as low as 6,850, implying downside of 8.5% from current levels.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

"Be cautious if a marginal new high toward ~7,741 occurs as it may be a 'bull trap' consistent with an expanding flat," wrote Ciana. A bull trap refers to a false breakout that reverses lower, while an expanding flat is a technical pattern where price swings get bigger over time instead of tightening.

Ciana Warns of Stretched Positioning as BofA Maintains Bearish Outlook

Ciana believes that price action appears "stretched," while optimism following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension is fading. In addition, Ciana notes that investors should take a defensive stance from July through September.

Earlier this week, Bank of America reiterated its 2026 S&P 500 price target of 7,100. The target implies downside of 5% and is the lowest forecast on Wall Street. The firm cited cooling liquidity drivers, including lower buybacks, dovish central banks, and falling institutional demand.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: AAL, DAL, UAL: Airline Stocks Rise as Analyst Sees Revenue Bucking Historical Trend
data.description:
Shares of leading U.S. carriers American Airlines (AAL), Delta Air Lines (DAL), and United Airlines (UAL) rose about 2% on Thursday morning. This came after BMO Capital Markets (BMO) raised its price targets on the shares. The firm noted that U.S. carriers are set to break a historical trend: maintaining high revenue even as jet fuel costs continue to fall.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

How the U.S.-Iran War Impacted Airlines

Earlier in the year, estimates suggested that airlines could be forced to cough up an extra $11 billion in jet fuel costs in 2026. This estimate emerged as the U.S.-Iran war triggered a surge in energy prices.

The development forced airlines to raise plane ticket prices. This sparked fears that air travel could slow down, hurting carriers' revenues.

Analyst Flags Revenue Retention Capacity

However, BMO Capital Markets analyst Michael Goldie on Wednesday noted that he expects total revenue per available seat mile (TRASM) to remain above historical levels even as jet fuel costs continue to fall. The analyst noted that, historically, TRASM usually declines when jet fuel costs drop. However, he sees capacity for carriers to defy the trend.

Defending the thesis, Goldie noted that demand for air travel has remained high even as fare increases have slowed. He believes that the weaker role of discount carriers, the rollout of more premium cabin services, and earnings beyond aircraft services will help airlines buck the trend.

How BMO Capital Now Rates the Airline Stocks

Based on these factors, Goldie raised the price targets of several airlines. Key highlights include:

  • American Airlines (AAL) — price target raised from $13.50 to $19.59, implying 8% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Hold rating is reaffirmed
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) — price target lifted from $80 to $105, suggesting about 13% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Buy rating is maintained.
  • United Airlines (UAL) — price target raised from $130 to $157.5, predicting about 17% upside from Wednesday's closing price. Buy rating is reiterated.

Who Is Michael Goldie?

Goldie is a four-star analyst who ranks in the top 22% of the more than 12,000 Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks.

He primarily covers the aerospace industry. The analyst boasts a 100% success rate and has generated an average return of 23% for investors based on his ratings.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Poaches Microsoft Executive to Lead Its 'Field Operations'
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has hired a senior executive from Microsoft (MSFT) to lead its field operations.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

In a statement, Nvidia said that it has hired Nicholas Parker as Executive Vice President of Worldwide Field Operations, effective August 24 of this year. Parker, age 55, joins Nvidia after spending the last 26 years at Microsoft, where he most recently served as Executive Vice President and Chief Business Officer in the Worldwide Sales & Solutions unit.

Parker also led Microsoft's partner sales and solutions teams during his tenure at the technology giant. Typically, field operations involve business activities and services performed away from company's central office. At Nvidia, this includes mobile teams, on-site technicians, and field sales representatives. Nvidia continues to expand sales of its microchips and processors around the world.

Parker's Compensation at Nvidia

Parker's compensation package includes an annual base salary of $1 million, a sign-on bonus of $5 million, and annual variable compensation of $1.5 million. He will also receive Nvidia stock options worth up to $35 million that vest over the next four years.

Parker's appointment follows the announced retirement of Ajay K. Puri, a 21-year Nvidia executive who notified the company of his intention to retire on June 28, 2026. Puri is expected to remain with the company in a senior advisory role during the transition.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 7/2/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Thursday as investors digest mixed jobs data.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The U.S. economy added 57,000 nonfarm payrolls last month, falling short of the estimate of 110,000. Furthermore, April's and May's payrolls were revised down by a combined 74,000 jobs. At the same time, the unemployment rate eased to 4.2% from 4.3% in May. That was below expectations of 4.3% and also marked a one-year low.

Payrolls Miss Fuels Interest Rate Relief

The nonfarm payrolls miss reduces pressure on the Fed to hike rates. At the next Fed meeting on July 29, the odds of a 25 bps hike dropped to 19.8% from 28.9% a day ago. Lower rates are generally a positive for stocks because they reduce borrowing costs and increase the present value of future earnings. They also encourage investment and growth.

Elsewhere, investors continue to rotate into beaten-down software names. Palantir (PLTR), Fair Isaac (FICO), and Adobe (ADBE) are all well in the green. Furthermore, AI-related stocks are showing mixed price action, highlighted by Intel (INTC), KLA Corp (KLAC), and SanDisk (SNDK).
data.author: Eddie Pan
